html:
The arms of House Fossoway of Cider Hall
The arms of House Fossoway of New Barrel

Apple are the edible fruits of apple trees.

Culture[ ]

Apples can be eaten by themselves or be prepared in cuisine, including baked apples,[1] dried apples,[2] apple crisps,[3] applecakes,[4] apple tarts,[5] stewed apples,[6] applesauce,[7] buns,[8] oatbread,[9] and in salads.[10][11] Apples are sometimes included in bowls of brown.[12]

Cider is a popular alcoholic beverage made from fermented apples.[13] Wine can be made from green apples.[14]

Apples are included in the sigils of House Fossoway of Cider Hall and House Fossoway of New Barrel.[3] Ser Derrick Fossoway, one of the Ninepenny Kings, was known as the Bad Apple.[15]

History[ ]

Foss the Archer, a legendary son of Garth Greenhand, is remembered for shooting apples off the heads of maids he fancied.[16] Rowan Gold-Tree, one of Garth's legendary daughters, is said to grown a tree from an apple wrapped in her golden hair.[16]

According to Septon Barth, Lady Florence Fossoway's bookkeeping skills came from "counting apples since she learned to walk".[17]

Queen Daenaera Velaryon was the target of an assassination attempt when the crusts of her apple tarts were poisoned.[18]

Ser Arlan of Pennytree often gave apples to Chestnut. When Ser Duncan the Tall sold Sweetfoot to Henly, he gave a few [[currency|coppers] back so that the horse could have some oats and an apple.[19]

Lord Randyll Tarly forbade his son Samwell from eating apples while at the Arbor after Lord Paxter Redwyne compared the youth to a pig with an apple in its mouth.[20]

Recent Events[ ]

A Game of Thrones[ ]

Lord Petyr Baelish eats an apple and tosses it into the Blackwater Rush while waiting for Lord Eddard Stark.[21] Eddard learns from Jon Arryn's former stableboy that Lord Arryn always brought carrots and apples for his horses.[22] Eddard sees farmers selling apples at the market near the River Gate.[22]

Baked apples fragrant with cinnamon are served during the Hand's tourney.[1]

Jon Snow has applecakes and blood sausage for breakfast at Castle Black.[4] Jon takes a small withered brown apple when he attempts to desert from the Night's Watch.[23]

Before killing the stallion which will be used for Khal Drogo's funeral pyre, Aggo feeds it a withered apple.[24]

A Clash of Kings[ ]

Lothor Brune eating an apple, by Alexandre Dainche © Fantasy Flight Games

Queen Regent Cersei Lannister recalls that the boar which had killed King Robert I Baratheon was served with mushrooms and apples at his funeral feast.[25]

Yoren has his young recruits gather apples from orchards.[26]

Apples are among the goods brought by the Myraham to Lordsport.[14]

Baked apples are served at the harvest feast in Winterfell,[27] and apple crisps are served at Bitterbridge.[3]

Remaining behind to protect his sworn brothers from wildlings in the Skirling Pass, Squire Dalbridge asks for his garron to be given an apple once it is home.[28]

Lothor Brune becomes known as Lothor Apple-Eater after capturing Ser Jon from House Fossoway of New Barrel and killing Ser Bryan and Ser Edwyd from House Fossoway of Cider Hall during the Battle of the Blackwater.[29]

Meera Reed finds a big sack of apples in the kitchens of Winterfell.[30]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

Traveling in the riverlands, Arya Stark, Gendry, and Hot Pie pass through a burned village in which a dozen men hang from apple trees.[31] Arya later throws a crabapple at Gendry.[32]

Bran Stark and his companions see a dozen apple trees among the inn at Queenscrown.[33]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

Alleras holding an apple, by elalendi ©

At the Quill and Tankard, Alleras uses apples thrown by Mollander for target practice.[13]

Brienne Tarth sees a grove of apple trees at the Quiet Isle.[34]

Ser Addam Marbrand's scouts find that some of Ser Daven Lannister's foragers have been hanged underneath a crabapple tree near Riverrun, with a crabapple shoved into each victim's mouth.[35]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

Lord Davos Seaworth speaks with an apple seller at Fishfoot Yard in White Harbor.[36]

Apples are among the rations given by the Night's Watch to free folk at Mole's Town.[37]

The ironborn of Asha Greyjoy drink Galbart Glover's apple wine.[38]

Quotes[ ]

He kept the apple for last. It had gone a little soft, but the flesh was still tart and juicy.[23]

—thoughts of Jon Snow

Pork's always best with applesauce, I find.[7]

—Eddison Tollett to Samwell Tarly

Tommen loves applecakes. Try not to let any sellswords make off with them.[39]

—Jaime Lannister to Boros Blount

Ben: I'm no maester, mind you, but I know you got to keep the bad apples from the good.
Daenerys: These are not apples, Ben. These are men and women, sick and hungry and afraid.[40]

—Ben Plumm and Daenerys Targaryen
